text: I feel a sense of curiosity and anticipation here. The transition from the sterile quiet of a modern conference or seminar room to the vibrant, intellectually charged energy of an exhibit on research and development evokes within me a philosophical wondering about the very nature of knowledge and progress. I feel keenly aware of how spaces, their colors, textures, and the arrangement of information shape not just what I learn, but how I feel as I engage with the world. A subtle excitement stirs in me—the possibility of new ideas waiting to be uncovered.
